rationale.completeness: Implements core memory profiling with configurable thresholds and warnings, but misses runtime reconfiguration support and has unbounded growth in global state structures (_warning_last_emitted dict grows indefinitely).
rationale.correctness: Has several bugs: should_emit_warning() incorrectly updates _warning_last_emitted even when returning False, global state is not thread-safe for parallel DAG processing, and config caching never invalidates on runtime config changes.
rationale.style: Mostly follows Airflow conventions with proper licensing, structlog usage, and config structure, but imports platform inside a function rather than at module level.
rationale.quality: Readable code with good dataclass abstractions, but relies heavily on module-level global mutable state which harms testability and thread-safety; the profiling and warning logic could be better separated.
